reasoning: The yfinance tool provided a combined statistical probability of 0.42 for the initial Jan 11 forecast, largely driven by slight short-term downward momentum and historically weak seasonal performance at this time of year. Given instructions to use this as a primary anchor but regress to 0.5 over time due to stock price unpredictability, my estimate for Jan 11 is 0.43. For the Feb 3 resolution date (roughly a month out), the short-term momentum signal decays, pulling the estimate closer to a random walk (0.47). By April 4 (three months out), the base rate of overall positive equity drift (around 2% per quarter, offsetting a likely ~$1.50 late-March ex-dividend) dominates short-term noise and begins to push the probability slightly above 0.5 (0.51). Upcoming Q4 2025 earnings (likely in early February) will introduce volatility but don't bias the expectation sufficiently away from standard drift to justify straying far from 0.5.
